symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetric non-blanching palpable purpura on both lower legs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Palpable purpura in a gravity-dependent lower-limb distribution is the defining feature of IgA vasculitis.
Any non-blanching rash obliges exclusion of meningococcal disease before benign causes are accepted.
Symmetric crops of small non-blanching lesions are not the pattern of mechanical bruising.

symptoms.symptom_name: Non-blanching quality on finger-press (patient-performed press test)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-blanching indicates extravasated red cells (purpura) rather than a blanching vascular erythema; patient-obtainable over video, admissible but unverified.
Non-blanching purpura is also the sign of a low platelet count and cannot be distinguished from vasculitis without a full blood count.
Being well and afebrile 12 hours in makes fulminant meningococcal sepsis much less likely, though not formally excluded remotely.
Skin-limited IgA vasculitis is typically not associated with systemic toxicity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rash confined to lower legs, sparing trunk/arms/face

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gravity/pressure-dependent lower-limb distribution is characteristic of IgA vasculitis.
Thrombocytopenic petechiae are usually generalised rather than confined to dependent areas.

symptoms.symptom_name: Upper respiratory tract infection about two weeks earlier

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: IgA vasculitis characteristically follows an upper respiratory infection by days to a couple of weeks.

symptoms.symptom_name: No abdominal pain/vomiting and normal-appearing urine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring but insufficient — microscopic haematuria/proteinuria requires urinalysis, so nephritis is not excluded by normal-looking urine.
Absence of GI involvement is consistent with skin-limited disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: No mucosal bleeding or bruising elsewhere

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lack of mucosal bleeding lowers the probability of significant thrombocytopenia, though a platelet count is still required to exclude it.
Arthralgia is common in IgA vasculitis but its absence does not exclude skin-limited disease.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal urine dipstick (no blood, no protein)

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Occult IgA nephritis (haematuria/proteinuria) is missed; this test cannot be performed over telehealth, which drives the need for in-person assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Urinalysis is the key test to detect/exclude renal involvement and to stratify prognosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal full blood count / platelet count

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Severe thrombocytopenia as the cause of non-blanching purpura is not excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal platelet count distinguishes vasculitic purpura from thrombocytopenic purpura.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal blood pressure and non-toxic appearance on clinician examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal blood pressure argues against significant nephritis; it is a clinician/device measurement not available over chat.
A non-toxic, well-perfused examination is strong in-person evidence against meningococcal sepsis — precisely what telehealth cannot provide.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Post-infectious dependent palpable purpura, systemically well
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: The combination of symmetric dependent palpable purpura, a preceding URTI and a well, afebrile state is the classic IgA vasculitis constellation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urine dipstick (urinalysis) for blood and protein
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Detects/excludes IgA nephritis; the single most important test for prognosis and cannot be done over telehealth.
Excludes severe thrombocytopenia as the cause of the non-blanching rash.
Screens for hypertension accompanying renal involvement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum urea, creatinine and electrolytes (renal function)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses renal function where nephritis is suspected.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Skin biopsy for direct immunofluorescence (IgA deposition)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not required acutely for a classic mild cutaneous presentation; reserved for diagnostic uncertainty and not a telehealth action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Broad autoimmune/vasculitis serology panel (ANA, ANCA, complement)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not first-line for a classic post-infectious cutaneous IgA vasculitis; over-ordering adds cost and false positives without changing acute management.
